definition: An unpleasant emotion caused by the threat of danger, pain, or harm
example: The only thing we have to fear is fear itself — nameless, unreasoning, unjustified terror which paralyzes needed efforts to convert retreat into advance.

definition: Extreme fear
example: The only thing we have to fear is fear itself — nameless, unreasoning, unjustified terror which paralyzes needed efforts to convert retreat into advance.

definition: Cause (a person or part of the body) to become partly or wholly incapable of movement
example: The only thing we have to fear is fear itself — nameless, unreasoning, unjustified terror which paralyzes needed efforts to convert retreat into advance.

definition: An act of moving back or withdrawing
example: The only thing we have to fear is fear itself — nameless, unreasoning, unjustified terror which paralyzes needed efforts to convert retreat into advance.

definition: An agreement entered into by two or more parties for their mutual benefit
example: I pledge you, I pledge myself, to a new deal for the American people.

definition: A very large quantity of something
example: The test of our progress is not whether we add more to the abundance of those who have much; it is whether we provide enough for those who have too little.

definition: The state of being well known for some bad quality or deed
example: Yesterday, December 7, 1941 — a date which will live in infamy — the United States of America was suddenly and deliberately attacked by naval and air forces of the Empire of Japan.
speech: Address to Congress requesting declaration of war

definition: The power or right to act, speak, or think as one wants
example: We look forward to a world founded upon four essential human freedoms.
speech: State of the Union Address (Four Freedoms)

definition: A collection of weapons and military equipment
example: We must be the great arsenal of democracy.

definition: A system of government by the whole population or all the eligible members of a state
example: We must be the great arsenal of democracy.

definition: The action of pacifying or placating someone by acceding to their demands
example: No man can tame a tiger into a kitten by stroking it. There can be no appeasement with ruthlessness.

definition: The quality of being cruel or having no pity
example: No man can tame a tiger into a kitten by stroking it. There can be no appeasement with ruthlessness.

definition: A feeling of uncertainty or lack of conviction
example: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow will be our doubts of today.
source: Undelivered Jefferson Day address (written the day before his death)

definition: The fulfillment or achievement of something desired or anticipated
example: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow will be our doubts of today.
source: Undelivered Jefferson Day address (written the day before his death)

definition: The state of being happy
example: Happiness lies not in the mere possession of money; it lies in the joy of achievement, in the thrill of creative effort.

definition: A thing done successfully with effort, skill, or courage
example: Happiness lies not in the mere possession of money; it lies in the joy of achievement, in the thrill of creative effort.

definition: The dependence of two or more people or things on each other
example: We have learned that we cannot live alone, at peace; that our own well-being is dependent on the well-being of other nations far away.

definition: Involving mutual assistance in working toward a common goal
example: The structure of world peace cannot be the work of one man, or one party, or one nation. It must be a peace which rests on the cooperative effort of the whole world.

definition: A person captured and kept confined by an enemy or criminal
example: Men are not prisoners of fate, but only prisoners of their own minds.

definition: The development of events outside a person's control, regarded as predetermined by a supernatural power
example: Men are not prisoners of fate, but only prisoners of their own minds.
